title: eriophorum cyperinum var. pedicellatum
intro: Eriophorum cyperinum var. pedicellatum, also known as cotton grass, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Cyperaceae, characterized by its distinctive cotton-like seed heads. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues and skin problems, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of cotton grass include soothing coughs, relieving asthma symptoms, and treating wounds and skin irritations. Cotton grass can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also used in folk remedies to make poultices for topical application.
